question.text: Which of the following categories of underlying assets includes wheat, cotton, and coffee?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Wheat, cotton, and coffee are agricultural products and fall under the agricultural commodities category of underlying assets for derivatives. **The Trap:** Energy products include crude oil and natural gas. Precious metals include gold and silver. Financial assets include stocks, bonds, and currencies. None of these categories include agricultural produce. **Cross-Exam Context:** Commodity derivatives are covered in NISM Series XVI (Commodity Derivatives). Series VIII focuses on equity derivatives but tests knowledge of all underlying categories. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Underlying assets are broadly classified into: (1) Agricultural commodities, (2) Metals, (3) Energy, and (4) Financial assets.
